subtasks.description: Query SAM (Software Asset Management) MongoDB collection to extract licensed user counts by license type and product, aggregated at account level
Query SQL consumption facts warehouse to extract active user counts by product and consumption period, including license type and seat allocation

Reconcile licensed user counts from SAM against active user consumption facts, calculate variance (over-provisioning, under-provisioning, optimal), and classify licensing posture per product with recommendations

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks required: (1) SAM query for licensed user inventory is a single mongo_query aggregation at small tier (basic grouping by license type/product); (2) SQL consumption query for active users is a single sql_gen at small tier (straightforward fact table filtering and aggregation); (3) Cross-reconciliation at mid tier because it requires joining two source outputs, calculating variance metrics, applying multi-product classification logic, and generating posture-specific recommendations. No policy_action needed—licensing classification is reconciliation output, not policy approval. No multitool needed—query targets standard SAM/SQL warehouse assets.

output.sql: SELECT cf.product, cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter, cf.license_id, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) as active_users, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) as total_seats, SUM(cf.amount_usd) as total_spend_usd, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.event_id) as event_count FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher = 'Microsoft' GROUP BY cf.product, cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter, cf.license_id ORDER BY cf.fiscal_year DESC, cf.fiscal_quarter DESC, cf.product
